title: Student Discipline: In-School Suspension
description: In-school suspension (ISS) allows the district to temporarily remove a student from regular classes while keeping them on campus and engaged in schoolwork. The policy outlines how ISS programs are set up, the conditions under which students may be placed in ISS, and what students are expected to do while assigned there. Parents and students can use this policy to understand their rights and what to expect if ISS is assigned as a disciplinary consequence.
